title: Trochocephalus caucasicus
intro: Trochocephalus caucasicus, also known as Caucasian wormwood, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Apiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Trochocephalus caucasicus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues and colds, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential liver toxicity.
